mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates typically support asset prices
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below its 10-year average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite a significant rise in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales combined with strong housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand, signaling a healthy economy that boosts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending and housing are key economic drivers
failure_condition: SPX returns are flat or negative in > 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates a rebound in manufacturing and supply chain activity, leading to improved corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX returns are not significantly positive during periods of accelerating INDPRO within a recovery regime (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation often leads to rate hikes
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite high and accelerating inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not positively correlated with PAYEMS monthly growth over any 5-year rolling window (correlation < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with rising interest rates suggests an overheating economy, which can lead to a market correction as investors anticipate aggressive monetary tightening to curb inflation, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:overheating economies often face monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX continues to generate positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods when both GDP growth is high and FEDFUNDS is rising significantly.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer confidence and spending, which directly impacts corporate earnings and signals a broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite a significant decline in retail sales.
